DELETE /accounts/{account_id}/workflows/.description: Deletes a Workflow. This only deletes the Workflow and does not delete or modify any Worker associated to this Workflow or bounded to it.

DELETE /accounts/{account_id}/workflows/.description: Deletes a workflow instance and its stored state.

GET /accounts/{account_id}/workflows/{wo.description: Retrieves logs and execution status for a specific workflow instance.

schema.description: Instance identifier. User-created instances match `^[a-zA-Z0-9_][a-zA-Z0-9-_]*$` (max 100 characters); cron-triggered instances can use a longer, system-generated id derived from the cron expression.

parameters.description: When true, omits step details and returns only metadata with step_count.
parameters.name: simple

schema.description: When true, omits step details and returns only metadata with step_count.

parameters.description: Step ordering: "asc" (default, oldest first) or "desc" (newest first).
parameters.name: order

schema.description: Step ordering: "asc" (default, oldest first) or "desc" (newest first).

delay.description: Specifies the delay duration. '[dynamic]' indicates the delay is computed by a user-supplied function.

sensitive.description: When set to 'output', step output is redacted from log and step output responses.

200.description: Get all logs and status from the instance.

GET /accounts/{account_id}/workflows/{wo.summary: Get logs and status from instance

GET /accounts/{account_id}/workflows/{wo.description: Retrieves the full, untruncated output for a specific step on a workflow instance. Returns a flat status-shaped JSON body with step `status` ('running' | 'waiting' | 'complete' | 'errored'), `error` (nullable), and `output` (the step value, or null while running/waiting/errored). When the step returned a ReadableStream from step.do, the response is served as 'application/octet-stream' with the raw bytes as the body instead of JSON. A `status='running'` response with non-null `error` indicates the step is currently retrying after a prior attempt failed.

schema.description: Instance identifier. User-created instances match `^[a-zA-Z0-9_][a-zA-Z0-9-_]*$` (max 100 characters); cron-triggered instances can use a longer, system-generated id derived from the cron expression.

parameters.description: Exact step name from the instance logs response, including the generated counter suffix.
parameters.name: name

schema.description: Exact step name from the instance logs response, including the generated counter suffix.

parameters.description: Step type to disambiguate step.do and waitForEvent entries that share the same name.
parameters.name: type

schema.description: Step type to disambiguate step.do and waitForEvent entries that share the same name.

parameters.description: Specific attempt number to retrieve output or error for.
parameters.name: attempt

schema.description: Specific attempt number to retrieve output or error for.

error.description: Error details when status='errored'; null otherwise.

output.description: Full step output or waitForEvent payload without truncation. Sensitive outputs are returned as '[REDACTED]'. Populated when status='complete'. May be a ReadableStream when the step returned one from step.do; stream outputs are served as application/octet-stream rather than JSON.

schema.description: Raw bytes of the ReadableStream returned from step.do.

200.description: Get the full output from a specific instance step.

400.description: Bad Request (e.g. 'attempt' parameter supplied with type='waitForEvent').

404.description: Step not found on this instance, or requested attempt is out of range.

429.description: Step output requests are rate limited.

500.description: Step completed but its stored output is corrupt or missing (server-side data-integrity failure).

schema.description: Instance identifier. User-created instances match `^[a-zA-Z0-9_][a-zA-Z0-9-_]*$` (max 100 characters); cron-triggered instances can use a longer, system-generated id derived from the cron expression.
